rulings.Case Title: SHARON ALLEN VS. RELATED MANAGEMENT CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To - Amendment To Complaint - And Order Thereon Substitute Defendants Correct Names [Civil Code. Procedure 473]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 29, 2023 line 1. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR AMENDMENT TO THE COMPLAINT is OFF CALENDAR. Insufficient notice. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHARON ALLEN VS. RELATED MANAGEMENT CO. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 29, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANTS RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LP AND LA SALLE PRESERVATION, LP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to file a complaint asserting and clearly labeling legally cognizable claims against the moving defendants. Plaintiff shall have 30 days from the notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. All parts of the amended complaint must be in clear and concise language and must be legible. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: SHARON ALLEN VS. RELATED MANAGEMENT CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEFENDANT STACEY SOLOMAN'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 (ANTI-SLAPP MOTION)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for August 29, 2023 line 1. DEFENDANT STACEY SOLOMAN'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 425.16 (ANTI-SLAPP MOTION) is GRANTED. Moving party met its burden under the first prong. On August 25, 2023 Plaintiff filed documents, which may be construed as an opposition. It is not clear if these documents have been served. This opposition would be untimely and does not appear to contain argument or evidence that would meet the requirements under the second prong of the Anti-Slapp statute. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
